project_profile.risk_profile: high (fork maintenance, security surface, streaming/sync concurrency)
project_profile.justification: Brief is a research+architecture+handoff mission over an existing large TypeScript/Bun monorepo (OpenCode), adding remote API, channel adapters, multi-instance isolation.

4-normalize.note: registers Draft; priorities provisional pending OQ-002

answers.OQ-001: instance = isolated runtime (own config/storage/cache/ports/secrets/logs; may serve multiple projects)
answers.OQ-002: MVP = remote API+SSE, cross-interface sync, Telegram adapter, multi-instance; WhatsApp post-MVP
answers.OQ-003: single operator; apps/channels use operator-issued API keys/tokens
answers.OQ-004: private network only (localhost/LAN/VPN); channels via outbound or webhook relay

log.event: MS-001 MET — PH-0 complete, PH-1 unblocked
log.note: EXP-001..004 all PASS (no FAIL -> no fallbacks, no Proposed DECs). Reports in research/experiments/ + ms-001-status.md. Design outputs: (1) no marid concurrency layer — upstream per-session Runner is the authority (EXP-001); (2) marid-instance env set = XDG_DATA/CACHE/CONFIG/STATE_HOME + OPENCODE_DB + allocated port + TMPDIR/TMP/TEMP (EXP-002); (3) Telegram >=2s edit cadence, 0x429, 222ms permission round-trip (EXP-003, live); (4) P-1 DROPPED — marid-auth attaches as outer wrapper on Server.Default.app.fetch, no server edit (EXP-004). Patch surface now P-2/P-3/P-CI only. Deferred live steps (bun-dependent, bun now installed): EXP-002 two-instance tree-diff (before marid-instance freeze), EXP-004 filtered build + binary + auth-wrapper smoke (before Gate-6). CI: timing-flake class fixed at root — centralized OPENCODE_TIMING_SCALE at test-wrapper choke points (P-CI-4); develop push run green first-try. PRs: #9 (PH-0 reports + P-CI/P-1 register), #10 (P-CI-4), #11 (develop->main sync, open), #12 (roadmap + keystone-state + CLAUDE.md operating manual). Toolchain: bun 1.3.14 installed (npm global; tree-sitter-powershell native build needs MSVC, use --ignore-scripts). NEXT: PH-1 / MS-002 (WBS-1.1..1.5) — marid profile build, marid-auth (tokens/scopes/rate-limit/audit/request-ID via the EXP-004 wrapper seam), v1 contract tests, branding P-2 + config P-3. Start prompt: handoff/follow-up-prompts.md 'PH-1 -> start'.

log.event: MS-002 MET — PH-1 complete, PH-2/PH-3 unblocked
log.note: PR #13 merged to develop (squash, all 11 CI checks green incl. 3-OS marid-build). MS-002: authenticated marid binary from the profile build passes contract tests. Built: (1) @marid/auth NEW dependency-free pkg — tokens (mar_ secrets, sha256-only, constant-time), scopes admin/client/channel-stub, token-bucket rate-limit (10/s burst30, SSE cap4, 429+retry-after), audit JSONL (+session), x-request-id echo; (2) marid serve wrapper on the EXP-004 seam (Server.Default.app.fetch, zero server edit) + disables upstream Basic auth so marid-auth is the single gate; marid token CLI; (3) TEST-CONTRACT pins v1 routes (Server.openapi()) + event taxonomy (EventManifest) + live /event SSE; (4) additive src/marid.ts entry + script/marid-build.ts profile build (binary compiled+smoked 3-OS) + hygiene grep. 92 new tests. Closes EXP-004 deferred live-build residuals. DECISIONS (DEC-011): (a) client-scope ownership DURABLE sidecar (survives serve restart), recorded on create+fork; (b) additive marid.ts entry over index.ts edit = P-ENTRY (sync-reconcile checklist added to upstream-sync-strategy.md); (c) branding split — identity (name+commands) in PH-1, cosmetic P-2 (README/TUI/UA/logo) + config P-3 (lsp:false) DEFERRED to PH-5. Patch surface now P-2(partial)/P-3(deferred)/P-CI/P-ENTRY. FOLLOW-UP flagged: firehose/list altitude limitation — wrapper can't body-filter global /event or GET /session, so client scope enforces session ACTIONS not OBSERVATION (deferred, options+trigger in open-decision-register.md). NEXT: PH-2 (marid-instance, MS-003) and/or PH-3 (cross-interface, MS-004) — both unblocked; PH-4 needs WBS-1.2 tokens (done). Start prompts: handoff/follow-up-prompts.md 'PH-2 -> start' / 'PH-3 -> start'.

log.event: Deferred firehose/list isolation RESOLVED (operator-directed) — not an MS/WBS, closes a registered deferred decision
log.note: Strict client-scope event/list isolation, built via option (b): a marid-owned filter in @marid/auth (src/event-filter.ts) wired into the middleware. ZERO upstream edit, no new P-* surface (all additive in the Marid-owned pkg). For any non-admin token the wrapper body-filters on the way out: drops non-owned SSE frames from GET /event (frame-parsed, buffered across chunk boundaries; session-less infra frames pass), and drops non-owned entries from GET /session (by id) + GET /permission (by sessionID). Admin never filtered. Key finding: every session event (v1 + v2) carries top-level sessionID (the event-sourcing aggregate key), so isolation is a one-field probe — pinned by a contract test that fails on upstream drift. Advisor-caught + fixed before ship: (1) invariant verified across ALL session families + pinned; (2) GET /permission was same leak class — now filtered; POST /permission/:id/reply residual (opaque per_ id) documented, not hidden; (3) upstream gzips JSON >=1KB — strip accept-encoding before delegating filtered list routes so the text-level filter isn't fed compressed bytes (verified live). Tests: marid-auth 42->62 (+20), opencode marid 50->52 (+manifest pin + live client-vs-admin isolation); typecheck + lint(0 err) clean. Register flipped Deferred -> Resolved. Residual reply-gating = future in-pipeline follow-up. PR #15 (merged to develop, squash a3524a6f9; synced to main via sync PR #16, merge-commit e14c232e1).

log.event: marid-instance BUILT (WBS-2.1..2.3) — local-validated on Windows; MS-003 exit pending 3-OS CI green
log.note: PH-2 started on operator's explicit `PH-2 -> start`. New dependency-free pkg @marid/instance (additive, DEC-009 — NO new patch surface): paths.ts (composeInstanceEnv = the EXP-002-verified isolation env set: XDG_DATA/CACHE/CONFIG/STATE_HOME + TMPDIR/TMP/TEMP; port isolated by --port 0; home intentionally NOT relocated; OPENCODE_DB omitted — XDG_DATA_HOME isolates the DB and hardcoding drifts vs opencode's channel-suffix logic), lifecycle.ts (detached launch via process.execPath + stdio->server.log; race-free port from the logfile readiness line; PID/port record; platform-split tree-kill: taskkill /T /F on Windows [no catchable SIGTERM], process.kill(-pid) SIGTERM->SIGKILL on POSIX; readiness rides OPENCODE_TIMING_SCALE P-CI-4; orphan-reaped on failed start; idempotent start guard — a second start on a live instance returns the existing record instead of spawning a second, un-tracked server [advisor-caught, WBS-2.2 'no orphan' DoD]; POSIX kill signals the group AND the bare pid so the server dies even if Bun's detached did not establish a group leader on linux/macos), registry.ts (add/list/remove + name-validation trust-boundary guard blocking path traversal; 0700 trees; registry = dir listing per ADR-0006). CLI: MaridInstanceCommand (add/list --json/path/start/stop/status/remove) added to marid.ts (Marid-owned entry — no new P-*). RISK-FIRST spikes (advisor-directed) BEFORE building: Windows detached-survival (verified cross-process) + taskkill /T tree-reap (0 strays) + logfile readiness — the two milestone-gating unknowns, cleared. Tests: @marid/instance 39 unit (every R-05 row table-driven; lifecycle via a real fake :0 server; registry+traversal) + live 2-instance diff instance-isolation.test.ts (MARID_ISOLATION=1) proving distinct ports, per-instance DB/sessions, cross-token 401, and the DEFERRED EXP-002 negative claim (no opencode state escapes the composed XDG roots into a shared fake home). Full CLI start/status/health(401 gated)/stop/remove validated end-to-end on Windows (post-stop = connection refused, no orphan). CI: new 3-OS marid-isolation job (ubuntu+macos+windows, MARID_ISOLATION=1, inherits unit-job Windows workarounds) + marid-instance unit step in the 2-OS unit job. opencode typecheck exit 0; lint 0 errors; marid-auth 62 pass + opencode marid 52 pass/1 skip unchanged. ADR-0006 now verified live (EXP-002 residual live-diff CLOSED). OPERATOR ACTION: the new `marid-isolation` required check must be added to the main+develop branch-protection rulesets (was 8 checks; becomes 9) — a ruleset edit only the operator can make; until then the job runs but does not gate. RIDER (same PR, 2026-07-05): two unit-windows flakes during #17 CI root-caused as P-CI-4 class, NOT PH-2 (different test each attempt; both pre-existing hardcoded budgets) — (a) acp stdin-EOF exit-wait 5s inline Effect.timeout -> x TIMING_SCALE; (b) core flock contention staleMs 1s crossed by CPU-starved holder under the 16-worker 2-core spawn stampede -> tolerance budgets x SCALE; (c) SYSTEMIC: turbo 2.x strict env mode was stripping OPENCODE_TIMING_SCALE from every test task except opencode#test — added to turbo.json globalPassThroughEnv (knob transport). Registers updated: upstream-sync-strategy P-CI-4 (+2 read-sites, +transport note), architecture.md P-CI row + P-ENTRY commands + ingress-altitude paragraph flipped to RESOLVED(PR #15), reconcile-checklist marid.ts bullet += instance. NEXT: open PR into develop; on 3-OS CI green, flip MS-003 MET here + in roadmap.md. PH-3 (cross-interface) also unblocked.

log.event: MS-003 MET — KPI-003 green on 3 OSes; PH-2 complete (merge of PR #17 pending operator instruction)
log.note: Exit evidence: 3-OS marid-isolation job (ubuntu 39s / macos 53s / windows) green on every PR #17 run — including the final all-14-green run 28740707282 on f7286921f AND green #2, an ANNOUNCED unit-windows job re-run (2 consecutive post-fix passes = the operator-approved validation bar; never silent retry-until-green). Devil's-advocate review (operator-directed) retro-gated the P-CI-4 flake fix and closed its residuals with two runtime probes: P1 — OPENCODE_TIMING_SCALE=0.01 through turbo made the core flock contention test die at the scaled 200ms cap, PROVING globalPassThroughEnv delivers the knob to core's task runtime (config-level dry-run evidence upgraded to runtime proof); P2 — TMP/TEMP marker dir received flock tmpdirs through turbo strict mode, so the D:\opencode-tmp environmental fix already reaches non-opencode packages via turbo's built-in allowlist (NO turbo.json TMP addition needed; U3 closed). Review deltas recorded: transport claim honesty (config vs runtime), bun file-order argument retracted (passed-at-least-once evidence carries the not-PH-2 conclusion), squash-history cost accepted (fix subject survives in squash body list), watch-list documented in upstream-sync-strategy P-CI-4 (no GH issue — solo-operator register suffices). Trackers flipped in this commit: roadmap PH-2 header + phase row + WBS-2.3 -> MS-003 MET. NEXT (operator): (1) merge PR #17 into develop — INV-005, explicit instruction only; (2) add marid-isolation to main+develop required checks (8->9); (3) PH-3 (cross-interface, MS-004) and PH-4 (Telegram, MS-005) both unblocked — start prompts in handoff/follow-up-prompts.md.
